SuperFAISS at core master commit e7323f4, tagged v3.3.0. v3.1.2 carried v3.0
channel-capable scratch banks + channel-scoped analytics + archive v3, v3.1 runtime-mutable
channel vocabulary (Relabel), v3.1.1 AVX2 float32 dot/L2 sub-8 segment-remainder fix, and
v3.1.2's own doc/version-header corrections — no kernel or behavior change since v3.1.1.

v3.2.0 delta — four new Tier 1 headers for Bank Inspector I, each dependency-free,
caller-provided buffers, deterministic with pinned tie-breaks, consuming exact query
output only (no kernel, quantization, or format change):
- `graph.h` — `BuildKnnNeighbors`/`MutualFilter`/`BuildDuplicateGroups`/`ConnectedComponents`,
  the mutual k-NN graph + connected-components mechanic behind the Structure view.
- `novelty.h` — `NoveltyScore`/`NoveltyProbeDistance`/`KthNeighborDistance`/
  `CalibrateNoveltyBaseline`, the two-limb (exact-identity + statistical-rank) novelty
  probe behind the Novelty view. `NoveltyProbeDistance`'s int8 leg is routed through the
  caller's `Workspace` (its quantized-probe staging is no longer a per-call heap
  allocation) and its channel leg rejects an off-grid channel range on a hand-built
  `BankView` (a validated bank's channel table is always grid-aligned, so this never
  fires on production data).
- `matching.h` — `MutualNearestMatches`, the sampled-A-over-full-banks CSLS matching
  behind the Correspondence view.
- `inspector_common.h` — `DequantizeRowAsQuery`, the shared row-to-query decode helper
  the three modules above and the plugin's own centroid-query path both build against.

All four headers are wired into the `superfaiss.h` umbrella header and documented in
`docs/API.md`.

Publish-gate corrections in this sync (commits ef25c5c, 6f43d62):
- Public headers, src, and tests cited the private records tree by path and named
  internal reviewer personas directly in shipped comments — stripped; the underlying
  technical content (invariants, disclosed limits, design rationale) is unchanged.
- The v3.0 channel-table contract ("fixed for the bank's lifetime"), repealed by v3.1's
  `Relabel`, was still asserted in `docs/API.md`, `docs/DETERMINISM.md`, `docs/FORMAT.md`,
  `scratch.h`, and `README.md` — corrected to the current mutable-via-Relabel contract.
- `inspector_common.h` was wired into the umbrella header but undocumented in `docs/API.md`
  — a full section added. Two further undocumented public free functions found by a new
  reverse doc-signature check (`Better` in topk.h, `ScoreChunkFused` in kernels.h) and one
  from an earlier release (`ValidateBiasPairs` in validate.h, `MaxNNCrossDeviceChannel` in
  analytics.h) were documented in the same pass.
- The README's "true 0.0 on Cosine" claim for `NoveltyProbeDistance` did not carry the
  qualifier `novelty.h`'s own header discloses (the guarantee is int8, or a byte-identical
  float32 row) — corrected to match `docs/API.md`'s already-correct scoping.
- The README's headline "Measured" table read as an ~8x single-thread win from batching
  alone; measured single-thread batch amortization is 1.16-1.44x, and the ~8x gap is host
  parallelism. The table now states threading explicitly per row and cites its bench
  source; `tests/bench_main.cpp` gained the table's own 40k x 100 workload so the
  single-threaded rows are reproducible from the core repo.

Publish-gate corrections in this sync (commit 4f4b5b6):
- The serial-scan figure (~0.5 ms) was superseded by two independent re-measurements
  (0.629 ms and 0.719 ms) settling on ~0.65 ms, corrected in the README's own table in an
  earlier pass, but the derived "~8x gap" statement and INTEGRATION.md's "10 GB/s-
  equivalent" bandwidth figure were still computed from the old number — recomputed to
  5.0x (chunk-parallel) / 10.8x (batched, host-parallelized) and ~6 GB/s-equivalent.
- "Three header-only Tier-1 modules" corrected to "Four" (`inspector_common.h` was
  omitted from the README's own count, though already documented and wired).
- `superfaiss_bench` (`tests/bench_main.cpp`) had no CMake target and no `build.bat`
  wiring — nothing in a fresh clone produced the executable the README's Reproduce
  column named. Added the CMake target and `build.bat` wiring; verified by a clean build
  producing and running `superfaiss_bench.exe`, reproducing ~0.65 ms on the 40k x 100
  int8 workload.

v3.2.1 delta — test suite only; `include/` and `src/` are byte-identical to v3.2.0:
- The kernel allocation cell referenced the `*ScalarAvx2` reference mirrors without an
  architecture guard, so `tests/test_main.cpp` failed to link on macOS arm64 (the mirrors
  are compiled out there) rather than failing a test.
- The header-only-math allocation cell sized its decode buffer to `dims` where
  `DequantizeRowAsQuery` writes `paddedDims` and zeroes the tail — an 8-float stack
  overrun on a 24-dim int8 bank, aborting the suite under glibc's stack protector on Linux.
  Confirmed with AddressSanitizer; the buffer is now sized from `constexpr PaddedDims`.
- Coherence checks (documented signatures match their declaring header, every public
  symbol documented, Markdown links resolve case-exactly) were added to the core repo's
  own CI. They previously ran only in this plugin repository against the vendored copy,
  so the core repo could publish a doc contradicting its own headers and stay green.

v3.3.0 delta:
- **`PeekScratchArchive`/`ScratchArchiveInfo`** (`scratch.h`, new) — reads a serialized
  scratch archive's header and channel table out of a byte span and reports its geometry
  plus `archiveBytes`, exactly the number of bytes a `Load` consumes, without allocating
  or reading the payload. Runs the same header validation `Load` does (the two now share
  one validator). Lets a host that appends its own trailer after the archive — this
  plugin's channel-name frame is one — locate and validate that trailer before
  committing the load, rather than discovering a broken trailer with the rows already
  replaced. Not yet consumed by this plugin.
- **`ComputePrincipalComponents`'s `scratch` parameter is now `double*`** (was `float*`,
  `pca.h`) — source-breaking. The covariance-apply accumulator now matches the mean's
  accumulation precision; a float accumulator rounded once per row and degraded the
  power iteration with bank size. This plugin's one call site
  (`SSuperFAISSBankInspector::ComputeProjection`, PCA scatter-plot projection) updated
  its scratch buffer from `TArray<float>` to `TArray<double>` in this sync.
- **`MeanNNCrossDeviceChannel`/`MaxNNCrossDeviceChannel` can now return `OutOfMemory`**
  (`analytics.h`) — both now stage a pre-lift of the target's non-excluded sub-rows into
  the caller's `Workspace` so each target's self-dot is computed once rather than once
  per source row; a failed reservation now surfaces as `OutOfMemory` instead of being
  unreachable. This plugin does not call either function directly.
- **`ScratchBank::Load` now validates the retention region** (`scratch.cpp`) — a
  retained row is by construction the post-normalization row the quantizer consumed, so
  `Load` replays the bake on each retained row and requires it to reproduce the stored
  row exactly. A fabricated-but-finite retained array previously loaded clean and handed
  `MeasureScratchRecall` an invented reference. Non-finite retained values are rejected
  the same way (`BadFormat`).
- **Segmented-scan / whole-row-scan kernel-selection disagreement fixed**
  (`kernels.cpp`) — `ResolveRowKernels`, which feeds the segmented scan and
  `DecomposeRowScore`, wired the float32 AVX2 dot/L2 kernels in directly with no
  sub-8-remainder rule, while the dispatchers `DotF32`/`L2F32` apply that rule. At
  `paddedDims` 20, 36, 52, 100, 132 and similar the two paths disagreed in the last ulp,
  falsifying the "no second code path exists to drift" decomposition guarantee. Fixed by
  routing `ResolveRowKernels` through the same dispatchers. `Exactness::CrossDevice` was
  never affected (int8 lengths are always multiples of 16); no pinned golden moves.
- **`ScratchBank::Create`/`Grow` geometry ceilings enforced before arena size is
  computed** (`scratch.cpp`) — `ArenaBytes` multiplies capacity by dims in signed
  `int64`; both entry points previously accepted any positive `int32` pair, so a large
  valid-typed request overflowed the arena computation (undefined behavior) rather than
  being refused. Both now apply the format's own ceilings (`kMaxBankRows` rows,
  `kMaxCrossDeviceDims` dims) before any size is computed, returning `InvalidArgument`.
  The archive loader already applied these caps; direct construction and `Grow` did not.
- **Bank-inspection review follow-ups** (`graph.cpp`, `analytics.cpp`, `pca.cpp`,
  `compose.cpp`) — `MutualFilter`/`ConnectedComponents` bound caller-supplied neighbour
  and duplicate-group values to `[0, count)` before indexing; the int8 off-grid channel
  guard is now shared between `novelty.cpp` and the channel-analytics legs;
  `ProjectRowsOntoComponents` bounds `componentCount` by `dims` like its sibling; scale
  decode goes through `detail::FloatBitsToDouble` uniformly so a subnormal scale decodes
  identically under any FTZ/DAZ mode.
- **A Cosine channel bank with zero rows was unrepresentable** (`validate.cpp`) —
  `ValidateBank` required the per-channel inverse sub-norm array whenever a Cosine bank
  carried channels; those norms are one per row, so a bank with no rows requires none.
  The rule now applies only when `count > 0` — the shape a channel-carrying scratch bank
  graduates into once every row has been removed no longer forces that graduation to
  drop its channels.
